marketState: neutral — bearish near-term weather offsets extreme 8-16d heat and crowded short COT
summary: Henry Hub front (Jul-26) trades at $3.228, sitting $0.27 above the fair-value midpoint ($2.958) but comfortably inside the $2.46–$3.46 band, so valuation is stretched but not alarming. The dominant near-term conflict is between soft 1-7 day demand (-5.8% vs 5y, rangePos 0.38) and an extreme 8-16 day heat forecast that sits above the 5-year maximum (+38.6%, aboveMax=true) — a rare signal that could ignite the fuel pile of crowded managed-money shorts at the 17th percentile of their 5-year range. Storage is the key structural bear: at 2,759 Bcf the surplus is +153 Bcf above the 5-year average and the latest injection beat estimates by 25 Bcf, capping rallies. Seasonality reliability is low (44pp 10-90 spread), so that signal is downweighted; the 4-week typical seasonal move of -2.3% and the 31% probability of being higher in week 4 both argue against a sustained summer rally, keeping long-term conviction low.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality is structurally bearish at the 4-week horizon (31% probability higher, typical move -2.3%) and storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average provides persistent injection pressure through shoulder season. The futures curve prices a flat summer ($3.22-$3.26 through October) with no market consensus for a sustained spot rally. The only compelling long case is the COT extreme-short positioning at the 17th percentile, but that is a trigger condition, not a directional signal in isolation — it requires a catalyst and a better entry price to overcome the structural headwinds.
longTerm.invalidation: Would consider a long if storage surplus erodes below +75 Bcf vs 5y, or if price retraces to the $2.85-$3.00 fair-value zone offering 1:3+ risk/reward. A short would become viable on a break below $2.92 (below fair-value midpoint) with sustained bearish storage data.
longTerm.trigger: Storage surplus narrows to within +75 Bcf of 5-year average, OR price retraces to $2.85-$3.00 with COT still at historically short levels — then reassess a contrarian long setup.

shortTerm.entry: $3.15-$3.22 (current market or on any dip toward $3.15 as 1-7d weather softness plays out)
shortTerm.stop: $2.98 (daily close below fair-value midpoint $2.958 invalidates the weather thesis)
shortTerm.tp2: $3.68 (extension beyond upper band on heat-wave confirmation and short squeeze)
shortTerm.rationale: The 8-16 day demand forecast is the clearest short-horizon catalyst: at +38.6% above the 5-year normal and above the historical maximum (aboveMax=true), markets typically begin pricing extreme heat 5-7 days in advance. COT managed-money positioning at the 17th 5-year percentile means the short base is historically crowded; any heat-driven demand spike risks a violent short squeeze. The 4h technical picture supports longs (bullish trend, 50>200 EMA, positive MACD, RSI 52) and intraday RSI at 39-44 on 5m-1h frames shows near-term softness without a trend break.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price closes below $3.00 on a daily basis, or the 8-16 day heat forecast reverts toward normal (rangePos drops below 1.0), or another bearish storage report beats consensus by more than 15 Bcf.
